programmes.show_description: Pair of Charlies. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie gets blown up-and let down. Based on a storyline by Jack Trevor Story.
1978. Sci-fi Comedy. Director: Norman Tokar. Stars Ken Berry, Sandy Duncan & Harry Morgan. A physicist and his girlfriend try to help an extraterrestrial space cat fix his ship and return home.
1949. Comedy. A nitwit investigator (Terry Thomas) goes undercover in a restaurant to catch a gang of crooks.
The Case of the Cunningham Heritage. 1954. Starring: Ronald Howard, H. Marion Crawford & Archie Duncan. Sherlock and Watson investigate a murder, with a young lady as the prime suspect.
1959. The Hotel. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1937. Adventure. Director: Edward A. Kull & Wilbur McGaugh. Stars Bruce Bennett, Ula Holt & Frank Baker. Tarzan, Martling and Ula retrieve a Mayan idol, leading them into chaos in the jungle!
1955. Starring Ruth Dunning, Edward Evans, Sidney James, Sheila Sweet, Peter Bryant, Nancy Roberts and Vera Day. Comedy based on the hugely successful TV series 'The Groves'.
1957. Crime Drama. A rogue smuggler enters a murky world when his wife is kidnapped. Directed by Maclean Rogers. Starring Hugh McDermott, Jane Hylton and Honor Blackman.
1953. Adventure. Directed by Robert D. Webb. Stars Robert Wagner, Terry Moore & Gilbert Roland. Fierce competition among sponge fishermen leads to a dangerous rivalry between two families.
1964. Enjoy this jukebox film of the wonderful Colette Renaud with her performance of the song Ah Dis Donc!
A fascinating look at the history of the prolific British film and television production company founded by the Danziger brothers and the studios they established to create features and series.
1957. Mystery. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars Kieron Moore, Jane Griffiths & Basil Dignam. Frank Martin is accused of murder and the only woman who can prove his innocence is missing.
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 1. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & David Ashford. Two mountaineers died in an unsuccessful attempt to scale the Choga Sar in the Himalayas.
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 2. 1974. Stars Keith Buckley, John Flanagan & Kenneth Cope. Captain Bowman claims that Wainwright lied about Nielsen dying in a fall...
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 3. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & Keith Buckley. Lloyd accuses the group of blaming Wainwright for Bowman's failures that lead to the two deaths.
1951. Adventure. Directed by Phil Karlson. Stars Barbara Hale, Richard Greene & Carl Benton Reid. A love affair between a farmer and an outlaw's daughter develops with bitter-sweet results.
The electrifying Screamin' Jay Hawkins performs the 1957 song Frenzy.
Dover Castle/The Psychic Healer. 1994. Michael Aspel introduces more unusual events, including paranormal activities at Dover Castle and Lorraine Ham, a remarkable healer who works with the NHS.
Full Circle. 1972. Stars Jean Anderson, Jennifer Wilson & Hilary Tindall. A positive future for Hammond Transport seems certain, but conflict within the family continues.
Murder by Error. 1958. Stars Donald Gray, Diana Decker & John Stuart. Vera thinks that her husband is being blackmailed so she calls Saber. An odd solution is found when blackmail turns to murder.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 26. 1975. Ronnie Hilton sings a number of hits, New World perform 'Kara, Kara' and 'Sister Jane'. David Copperfield, Valentino and Jimmy Marshall also star.
1953. War. Director: Brian Desmond Hurst. Stars Alec Guinness, Jack Hawkins, Muriel Pavlow, Anthony Steel & Flora Robson. British RAF face invasion on the strategic island of Malta during WW2.
The Plastic Eaters. 1970. Stars John Paul, Simon Oates & Robert Powell. Doomwatch faces its first challenge: to stop the spread of a man-made virus which has the power to dissolve plastic.
The Stockbroker's Clerk. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read where a young clerk consults Holmes with his suspicions concerning a company that has offered him a very well paid job.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Throwaway Society
programmes.show_description: 1968. A fascinating look at the different types of rubbish generated by society, and the problems of how to dispose of it, including recycling.
The Getaway - Part 1. 1974. Stars John Barron, Jonathan Elsom & John Flanagan. Phil McDowell is on trial for the abduction of Fiona Sumner, a fourteen year old girl. He denies the offense.
The Getaway - Part 2. 1974. Stars Lyndon Brook, Elizabeth Weaver & Tina Heath. Fiona Sumner tells the court she initially agreed to go with McDowell but later changed her mind.
The Getaway - Part 3. 1974 Stars John Flanagan, John Barron & Lyndon Brook. Son of Philip McDowell tells the court that Fiona wanted to go on the trip to visit her father who she thought was dead.
1958. War. Director: Vernon Sewell. Stars Michael Rennie, Patricia Medina & Christopher Lee. Polish Resistance discover a mysterious pilot-less plane.
1963. Who Killed the Kind Doctor? Directed by Don Taylor. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. An LA psychiatrist telephones Burke to warn him of an impending murder by one of his patients.
1960. The Show-Off. Stars Michael Ansara, Scott Marlowe & Joan Lora. A young man rides into town and allows everyone to believe he is Billy the Kid. Sam isn't sure if his claim is true or not.
1961. Crime. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Stars Gordon Jackson, Christina Gregg & Lisa Daniely. Tom's wedding day becomes a confusing nightmare when a stranger appears, claiming to be his wife.
1955. Comedy. Director: Vernon Sewell. A cockney family inherit a ramshackle farm. Alfie (Leslie Dwyer) wants to make a go of it. Starring George Cole and Kathleen Harrison.
1950. Drama. A lowly bank clerk marries a good time girl but she two-times him for her old crooked boyfriend (Sydney Tafler). Starring Jack Watling, Pat Kirkwood and Thora Hird.
Comedy. Director: David Paltenghi. Stars Arthur Askey, Glenn Melvyn & Thora Hird. Two football loving railway workers get into trouble after racing their engine home to get to a match on time.
Backfire. 1961. Directed by Paul Almond. Stars Alfred Burke, Zena Marshall, Oliver Johnston & Noel Treverthen. Everything points to prosperity in this luxurious factory! Or so it seems...
1963. Western. Director: Andrew V. McLaglen. Stars John Wayne, Maureen O'Hara & Patrick Wayne. Wealthy rancher McLintock uses his power and influence to keep the peace between conflicting groups.
The Fine Print. 1960. Stars John Ireland & Nora Nicholson. A woman is unable to claim the money from her late father's insurance policy after he forgot to read the fine print. Can anyone help her?
1946. Drama. Directed by Robert Hamer. Stars Googie Withers, Mervyn Johns & Gordon Jackson. Victorian Brighton; the tale of a pious chemists' household & the underworld occupied by landlady Pearl.
1959. Drama. A burglar murders an old woman but a blind girl 'witnesses' the robber as he flees. Stars Patricia Dainton, Conrad Phillips, Nigel Green & Richard O'Sullivan.
On Watch - Newtown. 1962. Stars Joseph Brady, Brian Blessed & Jeremy Kemp. Barlow is searching for counterfeit money, meanwhile Steele and Lynch are tracking Ralph before he kills his father.
Caroline Munro is back to open the Cellar Club for another night of horror! Kicking off the show tonight is the fourth episode of Supernatural starring Jeremy Brett: Mr Nightingale.
Mr. Nightingale. 1977. Stars Jeremy Brett, Lesley-Anne Down & Susan Maudslay. An Englishman travels to Hamburg for business but finds a mysterious double is haunting him.
Up next, Caroline Munro prepares you for the 1960s horror film Devil's Corner.
1960. Horror. Director: Charles R. Rondeau. Stars Edgar Buchanan, Jean Allison & Richard Crane. An old man sells his soul to the devil to become young and win a woman from his rival.
Caroline Munro closes tonight's show and covers what to expect next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: The Late Nancy Irving. 1984. Stars Cristina Raines & Marius Goring. A victim of a car accident finds herself in hospital and discovers that she is not a patient but a prisoner...
